purpose: Adversarial consistency review; never used to mutate or authorize demo state.

risks: The embedded resolution can be mistaken for observed run history even though the recorded run remains blocked at revision 7.
Presenting the resolution events, revision 8, or completed nodes as actual would falsely imply that an intervention was authorized or executed.
The phrase “retrying infrastructure would create risk” is directionally plausible but the packet only proves that retry is not the indicated remedy; it does not establish a specific resulting harm.
review.suggested_copy: Observed: This synthetic run is blocked at revision 7 awaiting named-operator approval for the write-capable deploy.release request. The queue lease is healthy, the claim remains fenced, and no external effect has occurred. Simulation preview only: If approve_tool_call is accepted while the revision still equals 7, the demo would record a local simulated effect, advance to revision 8, and mark the run completed. No network request or real deployment is represented.
supported_claims: The current causal blocker is the approval node, not the terminal node.
A write-capable deploy.release request for staging-eu-1 reached a human approval boundary.
The run is blocked at revision 7 and the approval decision is pending.
The queue lease is healthy, has a fenced claimant, and had 42 seconds remaining at the recorded heartbeat.
The effect and terminal transitions are pending and unreachable until approval is resolved and the effect transition is acknowledged.
No external effect is recorded in the observed state.
The intervention contract permits approve_tool_call only from blocked state with an expected revision of 7.
Any contemplated effect is explicitly synthetic, local, and scoped to synthetic staging.
unsupported_claims: That build-week-judge actually authorized a command.
That the tool request was actually authorized or completed.
That a synthetic effect was actually recorded.
That the run actually reached terminal or revision 8.
That an idempotent command receipt was actually appended.
That the cause node is terminal in the observed run.
That the intervention was acknowledged and the run completed.
That a receipt can currently be inspected as an observed artifact.
That retrying infrastructure would definitely create risk, rather than merely being unsupported as the remedy.
